input.prompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects that currently run on VSTest.
I want to switch all of them to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. We're on .NET 8 SDK
and use `dotnet test --logger trx` in CI. What changes do I need to make to each
project and to the CI command? I want properties set consistently across all projects.

data.content:
Here's what you need:

## 1. `Directory.Build.props` (shared across all test projects)

Add a single property to enable the new platform and TRX output:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

| Property | Why |
|---|---|
| `EnableMSTestRunner` | Switches from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner |
| `OutputType` → `exe` | Required — each test project becomes a self-contained executable |
| `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` | Bridges `dotnet test` to the new platform (needed on .NET 8; default-on in .NET 9+) |

Place this file in the common ancestor directory of your three test projects so it applies to all of them automatically.

## 2. Per-project `.csproj` — no changes needed

MSTest 3.8.0 already ships the `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` libraries, so no new package references are required. The `Directory.Build.props` handles everything.

## 3. CI command change

```bash
# Before (VSTest)
dotnet test --logger trx

# After (Microsoft.Testing.Platform)
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory $(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory)/TestResults
```

The command itself stays the same — `--logger trx` still works thanks to `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`. The only thing to watch:

- The new runner writes one TRX **per test project** into each project's output directory by default. Use `--results-directory` to collect them in one place.

## Verify

After the changes, run:

```bash
dotnet test --logger trx
```

You should see output like `Microsoft Testing Platform v1.x` (not `VSTest`) at the top of each test project's run, confirming the switch worked.
data.reasoningText: The user wants to migrate 3 MSTest 3.8.0 test projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform on .NET 8 SDK. Let me provide a clear, concise answer based on my knowledge of this migration.
